title: Gleichenia velata
taxonomy: Gleichenia velata is classified in the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Gleicheniales, family Gleicheniaceae, and genus Gleichenia. It belongs to the order Gleicheniales, which is a group of ferns that are closely related to the order Matoniaceae. The family Gleicheniaceae is a small family of ferns that are primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The genus Gleichenia includes about 10-15 species of ferns, all of which are found in the Old World. The classification of Gleichenia velata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of the Pteridophyta division.

intro: Gleichenia velata, also known as the Black Renga Fern, is a type of flowering plant and a member of the fern family (Gleicheniaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and fever, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Gleichenia velata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
